chemistry.reaction_name: Photochemical protein conjugation via aryl azide photoactivation

photocatalyst.name: No external photocatalyst - substrate is photoactive
photocatalyst.role: The aryl azide group in the substrate acts as the photoactive component

chemistry.bond_formed: C-N bond between nitrene intermediate and protein amino acid residues

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Highly reactive nitrenes formed from aryl azide photolysis, which react to form seven-membered azepine ring species

chemistry.scope_summary: Proof-of-concept study demonstrating microfluidic photochemical preparation of 89Zr-radiolabelled protein conjugates using photoactivatable DFO derivatives with aryl azide groups. The method achieved 31.2 ± 1.3% radiochemical yield in flow vs 59.6 ± 3.6% in batch reactions, both with >90% radiochemical purity.

figures.key_information: Multi-step synthetic scheme showing conversion of starting material with amino group to final product DFO-PEG3-Et-ArN3 through 5 reaction steps (a-e) with yields of 76%, 82%, 76%, 87%, and 58% respectively
figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Synthesis of DFO-PEG 3 -Et-ArN 3  (1). Reagents and conditions: (a) imidazole-1-sulpho- nyl azide HCl, CuSO 4 ⋅ 5H 2 O, K 2 CO 3 , MeOH, rt, 18 h; (b)  N -Boc-4,7,10-trioxa-1,13-tridecanediamine  ( 3 ), HATU, DIPEA, anhyd. DMF, rt, 24 h; (c) TFA, CH 2 Cl 2 , rt, 1 h; (d) succinic anhydride, anhyd.  DMF, rt, 48 h; (e) DFO-mesylate, HATU, DIPEA, anhyd. DMF, rt, 48 h.

figures.flow_path: Solution A and Solution B feed into photoconjugation reactor, then flow reaction leads to product
figures.key_information: Photoconjugation reaction between radiolabelled ligand with photoactive group and native protein under light irradiation
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Illustration depicting the general concept of the light-activated preparation of  89 Zr-radio- labelled protein conjugates in a microfluidic photochemistry device.
Scheme 2.  Preparation of the [ 89 Zr]ZrDFO-PEG 3 -Et-azepin-HSA protein conjugate via sequential  radiolabelling and flow photoconjugation.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Batch photoconjugation in glass vials lacks precise control over reaction conditions and may have less efficient light penetration
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous flow processing with controlled residence time and uniform light irradiation in transparent microfluidic reactor
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor geometry changed from glass vial to microfluidic chip with defined channel dimensions and dual-sided LED illumination
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photochemical mechanism (aryl azide photoactivation), same reagents, same product
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Reduced handling of radioactive materials through automated flow processing

figure_descriptions: Multi-step synthesis scheme for DFO-PEG3-Et-ArN3 precursor
Conceptual flow reactor diagram showing dual-inlet photoconjugation setup
Sequential radiolabeling and photoconjugation mechanistic scheme

notes: This is a proof-of-concept radiochemistry study demonstrating microfluidic photoconjugation for preparing 89Zr-labeled protein conjugates. The flow method achieved lower yield (31.2%) compared to batch (59.6%) but offers advantages in automation and safety for radioactive material handling. The study lacks extensive optimization data.
